comment: Default-FAIL verification contract for T-003 (medium, acceptance-first). Populated after the quality gate reran tests/guard-ps1-ascii.tests.sh (9/9, all three TARGETS entries) and re-verified domain-review-precheck.ps1's two required reject-path conditions byte-for-byte against the unedited domain-review-precheck.sh original, across named-parameter AND production array-splat calling conventions (4/4 parity checks pass) -- and pwsh tests/loop-consistency.tests.ps1 (27/27 passed, 0 SKIP, 0 FAIL). This supersedes the implementation report's own PARTIAL PASS finding: at gate time, host commit 9ca31bb ("fix(tests): loop-driver.ps1 --argjson manifest splat on multi-line jq output", landed after T-003's report and before T-004 began) is already an ancestor of HEAD, so the previously-documented pre-existing Publish-LoopDomainRoundBContract defect that FAILed TEST-008.12 no longer reproduces -- the domain leg drives fully green. Shell stack waives the three compile-oriented checks (lint/typecheck/build); this task ships one new PowerShell script (a full-parity port of an unedited .sh original) plus a hygiene-suite generalization, no compiled toolchain.

checks.waiver_reason: No lint tool configured for this repository's shell/PowerShell test-infrastructure files (stack: shell); bash -n and pwsh parse validity are exercised implicitly by every green run recorded in focused-tests.log.
No static type-check command is configured for shell/PowerShell test-infrastructure files.

The port is exercised end to end through the REAL loop-driver dispatch and the REAL loop-consistency domain leg against isolated mktemp fixtures; no separate service-integration surface exists beyond what unit/acceptance already exercise.
No compile/build toolchain for shell/PowerShell test-infrastructure files.
No runnable UI/service; this task adds only a full-parity PowerShell port plus a hygiene-suite extension, fully covered by the focused and regression logs.

No baseline-behavior.md; this is not a formally classified refactor/bugfix task so differential-test-policy.md does not apply verbatim. Left required:false, but the task's own byte-for-byte reject-path comparison against the unedited .sh original (recorded in focused-tests.log) is the functionally closest analog to a baseline diff -- it detects any degraded or loosened port automatically.
No user-facing UI surface; this task adds only plugins/sdd-domain/scripts/domain-review-precheck.ps1 plus a tests/guard-ps1-ascii.tests.sh TARGETS entry.
ds_profile: none; no design-system/ directory exists in this repository.
